name: The 64-bit Tipping Point: Optimizing Performance, Flexibility, and Value with Intel Itanium Architecture and Intel EM64T
description: Intel's September 2004 white paper positions two complementary 64-bit architectures—Itanium for high-end business-critical applications and Intel Xeon with EM64T for general-purpose computing—as the optimal 64-bit migration path. Citing IDC forecasts of Itanium server market growth from under $1 billion (2003) to $8 billion (2008), and Aberdeen Group's Peter Kastner on Itanium outperforming RISC, the paper argues that EM64T will trigger broad mainstream 64-bit migration while Itanium holds the data-tier niche. BEA WebLogic and SAS case studies illustrate platform selection tradeoffs.
